column2: Machine learning is a subfield of artificial intelligence focused on building systems that learn from data.
Deep learning uses neural networks with many layers to learn hierarchical representations of input data.
Natural language processing combines linguistics and machine learning to help computers understand text.
Transformers are a neural network architecture that uses self-attention to model long-range dependencies in sequences.
BERT is a pre-trained transformer model commonly fine-tuned for downstream NLP tasks.
Reinforcement learning trains agents to make decisions by maximising cumulative rewards from an environment.
Computer vision enables machines to analyse and interpret images and videos.
Convolutional neural networks are widely used for image classification and object detection tasks.
Python is a high-level programming language popular for data science and scientific computing.
JavaScript runs in web browsers and powers most interactive front-end web applications.
Docker packages applications and their dependencies into portable containers that run consistently across environments.
Kubernetes orchestrates containerised workloads, automating deployment, scaling and rollouts.
REST APIs expose backend services over HTTP using stateless request-response interactions.
GraphQL gives clients a flexible query language to request exactly the data they need from an API.
PostgreSQL is an open-source relational database management system known for standards compliance and extensibility.
Redis is an in-memory data store often used for caching and pub/sub messaging.
Continuous integration runs automated tests on every commit to catch regressions early.
Continuous delivery extends CI by automating deployments so changes can ship to production safely and quickly.
Sentence embeddings map sentences to fixed-length vectors so that semantically similar sentences land near each other.
Vector databases index high-dimensional embeddings to support fast approximate nearest-neighbour search.
